conversation_text:
Persuader: Hi there!
Persuader: I am looking for donations for the Save the children's Foundation?
Persuader: Could I interest you in that?
Persuadee: I would perhaps be interested can you tell me more a bout it
Persuader: It is a charity that helps hungry children in need.
Persuader: It is a international, non governmental organization that promotes children's rights, provides releif and support children in developing countries.
Persuadee: That sounds great, What countries do they help children in?
Persuader: There's quite a few! It's international.
Persuader: Even the smallest donation would make a difference to a child in need.
Persuadee: Ok I would like to see if I can help. I love children.
Persuader: That's great!
Persuader: They are the future you know...
Persuader: alot of the children are in war zones and facing daily threats of violence.
Persuadee: Of course nothing gets me more angry then seeing a child warrior soldier.
Persuadee: Will we also be helping children soliders?
Persuader: There are no children soliders, only children that need help in some way with food, resources, emergency response, etc.
Persuader: This program has saves a great deal of lives.
Persuader: It has brought families together and gave hope to a new life.
Persuadee: Ok thats great.
Persuadee: Do they need clothes as well?
Persuader: Yes! Alot of the proceeds go towards garments, shoes, and other things for the children.
Persuader: Some of them are basically naked and have nothing.
Persuader: Others have no shoes.
Persuader: It's really sad, but that's why there are programs out here like us to step in and provide help.
Persuadee: Ok well I know it is important for them to have basic needs.
Persuadee: I hope the money donated goes directly to them and not in the pockets of those running the organization.
Persuadee: Are you certain that the money will be used the right way?
Persuader: 86.5% of it goes towards support program services.
Persuader: And it is also tax deductible
Persuadee: That sounds like a good percentage and Im glad it will give me a tax break as well.
Persuadee: How do you receive payments?
Persuadee: credit card? cash?
Persuader: You can donate stock or mutual funds, you can mail it in to a designated address, you can make a payment online with a debit or credit card, PayPal is also an option, there are a ton of ways to get it done!
Persuadee: Wow that is a lot of options.
Persuadee: What would you say is the amount of a the common donation that  someone like myself would donate.
Persuadee: I am ready to donate today and would like to know an acceptable donation.
Persuader: Anything is acceptable!
Persuader: Whatever you feel comfortable!
Persuader: Just keep in mind that the children out here need you!
Persuader: I would suggest $20 a month if you can! If not, whatever you can spare.
Persuadee: Ok I would like to donate the $20 a month but I would like to hook it up to a credit card that is automatically deducted
Persuader: That's totally fine!
Persuader: Please visit us at  URL to read more and make your first donation!
Persuadee: Ok, thank you so much for your help.
Persuadee: You have made this a good learning experience and I feel confident in my donation to Save The Children.

conversation_text:
Persuader: Hello, have you heard of Save The Children?
Persuadee: I think so, it sounds familiar.
Persuadee: it is a nonprofit charity if i recall.
Persuadee: What exactly do they do?
Persuader: Save The Children helps kids from around the world by taking contributions from generous patrons. Kids from countries that have been stricken by poverty, violence, and other blights.
Persuadee: What countries has the charity worked in recently?
Persuader: Well, we have programs set up in 120 countries in total right now.
Persuader: Some of our most recent campaigns involve Myanmar - in Rohingya where parents and their children have been forced to flee because of violence to surrounding countries. In the largest refugee camp stationed in Bangladesh, there are an estimated 915,000 children and adults taking shelter.
Persuadee: Wow that sounds like a really robust program.
Persuadee: How long has Save the Children been around?
Persuader: It was founded in 1919, on April 15th in London - UK.
Persuadee: Do you work for Save the Children or do you just volunteer for the charity?
Persuader: I do not work for them, I work on behalf of them.
Persuader: That is why I'm talking to you today.
Persuader: These children need help, and could use it from generous people like you.
Persuader: A meal, an education, an opportunity like everyone else, that is all these kids want.
Persuader: Even one cent will add up to help a child in need, but if you wish you may donate up to 2 dollars.
Persuader: For every cent you donate it will come directly from your task payment.
Persuadee: I love to help charities that serve a good cause and I would love to donate but I do have one question.
Persuadee: What is the portion of donations that goes to children versus the portion that goes to administration or other expenses
Persuader: Sure, absolutely. In 2017, 86% went to Program services - or services that directly help aid these children. 9% went towards fundraising efforts, and 5% went towards our Management & other general costs. In 2018 we anticipate similar numbers.
Persuadee: That's pretty impressive!
Persuadee: Not too many charities can claim such a strong commitment to the cause.
Persuader: We do it for them, not for ourselves.
Persuader: And we hope our numbers reflect that amount of love and effort that goes toward what we do.
Persuadee: I'd be willing to donate $1 to Save the Children.
Persuader: That is very kind!
Persuader: After this chat is complete, you may choose how ever much you wish to give on the next page.
Persuader: Would you like to know about a potential child this money would be helping?
Persuadee: Sure, tell me more about a potential child this money would help.
Persuader: Mim, in Bangladesh is 7 years old, and wants to become a teacher.
Persuader: It could go towards her education, because in her country the average girl only stays in school until 10 years old.
Persuader: But more likely, it will be going towards meals or daily living expenses, with the average parent from her country only earning $3 a day.
Persuadee: How much does a $1 dollar donation change a child like Mim's situation?
Persuader: As you can see, $1 will change their world.
Persuader: One more meal or a pair of shoes will be one less thing that Mim, and her parents need to worry about.
Persuader: Bangladesh has been one of the largest parts of that Rohingya refugee exodus from Myanmar that I mentioned before and have been struggling themselves taking on more than 655,000 extra people.
Persuader: This has caused further hardship on an already hardened country.
Persuadee: Besides donating and raising awareness, is there any other way I can help Save the Children?
Persuader: Yes.
Persuader: You can slo sign up & stay connected on our website with your email and phone number.
Persuader: You can also volunteer if you're in the area of any of these children at our program centers.
Persuader: Thank you for donation, there will never be enough people like you in the world!
coaching_feedback:
OVERALL PERFORMANCE:
This was a successful persuasion conversation -- the persuadee donated $1.00. The persuader maintained a positive tone throughout and guided the persuadee toward a donation decision effectively.

WHAT WORKED:
- Incorporated logical arguments and factual information about the cause, helping persuadees who prefer evidence-based reasoning.
- Made emotional appeals to highlight the impact on children, one of the most effective charity persuasion strategies.
- Established credibility by providing factual information about Save the Children's mission and track record.
- Clearly explained the donation procedure, reducing friction and increasing the persuadee's confidence.
- Elicited positive responses including: ask-org-info, positive-reaction-to-donation, agree-donation, provide-donation-amount.

AREAS FOR IMPROVEMENT:
- No major weaknesses identified.

SPECIFIC SUGGESTIONS:
1. This conversation is a model. Key elements: logical-appeal, donation-information, emotion-appeal.
2. Document which turn led to agreement and replicate that approach.
